revision.text:
[[am:ጳጉሜ]][[ar:نسيء (شهر مصري)]][[da:Pi Kogi Enavot]][[de:Heriu-renpet]][[en:Intercalary month (Egypt)]][[ext:Pi Kogi Enavot]][[ko:파구메]][[lt:Pi Kogi Enavot]][[no:Pi Kogi Enavot]][[pl:Dni epagomenalne]][[zh:閏月 (埃及)]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME = D2:D21-G43 M4:X1
Heriu-renpet
''Ḥrjw-rnp.t''
''Between the Years'' /
''(Days) That Are Over the Year'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|en|Old Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q177819|lt=Old Kingdom|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt}} }} '''Heriu-renpet''' referred to specific additional days in the [[:en:Ancient Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]], known as [[:en:Epagomenal days|Epagomenal days]]. In the early {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|en|Old Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q177819|lt=Old Kingdom|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt}}, these five intercalary days were not yet associated with specific {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|en|List of Egyptian deities|qid=Q1076616|lt=Egyptian deities|1=List of Egyptian deities}}, as the [[:en:Ancient Egyptians|Egyptians]] believed these days were ruled by the [[Chatiu demons]]. {{ill|Plutarch|en|Plutarch|qid=Q41523|lt=Plutarch|1=Plutarch}} (''Is. et Os.'' 12) explains that {{ill|Thoth|en|Thoth|qid=Q146921|lt=Thoth|1=Thoth}} ({{ill|Hermes|en|Hermes|qid=Q41484|lt=Hermes|1=Hermes}}) won these five days in a game against {{ill|Khonsu|en|Khonsu|qid=Q190521|lt=Khonsu|1=Khonsu}} ({{ill|Selene|en|Selene|qid=Q131585|lt=Selene|1=Selene}}), enabling him to grant them to [[Nut (Egyptian mythology)|Nut]] ({{ill|Rhea (mythology)|en|Rhea (mythology)|qid=Q108419|lt=Rhea|1=Rhea (mythology)}}). She required these days to give birth to her children, some of whom were conceived in adultery, as her husband [[Geb (Egyptian mythology)|Geb]] ({{ill|Cronos|en|Cronos|qid=Q407374|lt=Cronos|1=Cronos}}) had cursed her. == Background == === Old and Middle Kingdoms === [[File:Sekhmet.svg|thumb|upright|Sekhmet as a dangerous goddess of plagues]] Heriu-renpet was originally closely linked to the month of [[Wepet-renpet]] and the goddess {{ill|Sopdet|en|Sopdet|qid=Q930167|lt=Sopdet|1=Sopdet}}, embodied by the star {{ill|Sirius|en|Sirius|qid=Q3409|lt=Sirius|1=Sirius}}. From {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|en|Predynastic Egypt|qid=Q714601|lt=predynastic times|1=Predynastic Egypt}} to the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}}, Heriu-renpet represented a time around early {{ill|June|en|June|qid=Q120|lt=June|1=June}}. By the start of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}}, it corresponded roughly to June 27–July 3. In the Old Kingdom, these intercalary days were tied to the [[:en:Nile inundation|Nile inundation]] and the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|qid=Q770568|lt=Achet|1=Season of the Inundation}} season, placed between the month of [[Ipet-hemet (month)|Ipet-hemet]] as the "closer of the year" and Wepet-renpet as the "opener of the year." During the [[:en:5th Dynasty of Egypt|5th Dynasty]], with the introduction of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|qid=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}} worship, the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|en|Pyramid Texts|qid=Q645882|lt=Pyramid Texts|1=Pyramid Texts}} first linked these days to specific deities. The sky goddess [[Nut (Egyptian mythology)|Nut]] was said to oversee Heriu-renpet, serving as the wife of [[Geb (Egyptian mythology)|Geb]] and the "mother of the (five) gods." The eldest of these gods, Osiris, was killed by his brother [[Seth (Egyptian mythology)|Seth]]. Nut was said to "light her torches as {{ill|Star|en|Star|qid=Q523|lt=Star|1=Star}}s" to guard the heavens. The Egyptians considered these five intercalary days to be part of the {{ill|Creation myth|en|Creation myth|qid=Q215304|lt=Creation myth|1=Creation myth}}, critical to maintaining cosmic order. The [[Heliopolitan Ennead]] linked the days to sacrifices for Seth to preserve the [[Maat (Egyptian mythology)|Maat]]. The days were also associated with {{ill|Sopdet|en|Sopdet|qid=Q930167|lt=Sopdet|1=Sopdet}} and her [[:en:Memphis (Egypt)|Memphite]] counterpart, {{ill|Sekhmet|en|Sekhmet|qid=Q146104|lt=Sekhmet|1=Sekhmet}}: {{Quote|Sekhmet’s messengers carry {{ill|pestilence|en|pestilence|qid=Q7171505|lt=pestilence|1=Pestilence}} across the lands. Those who know the names of the intercalary days will neither hunger nor thirst. They will not fall victim to the yearly plague. Sekhmet will have no power over them. Do no work on grain or clothing during these evil days, nor begin any task.}} Texts from the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}}, such as the {{ill|Rhind Mathematical Papyrus|en|Rhind Mathematical Papyrus|qid=Q213540|lt=Rhind Mathematical Papyrus|1=Rhind Mathematical Papyrus}}, begin to assign Heriu-renpet to specific deities. For example: - "On the day of Seth's birth, he thunders." - "On the day of {{ill|Isis|en|Isis|qid=Q79876|lt=Isis|1=Isis}}’ birth, the sky rains (tears)." The ''Book of the Intercalary Days'' includes additional attributions: 1. Osiris: "Festival of Osiris, who is like the {{ill|Nile perch|en|Nile perch|qid=Q654130|lt=Nile perch|1=Nile perch}} in his pond." 2. Horus: "Festival of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|qid=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}, the pure bull in his fields." 3. Harakhty: "Festival of [[Ra-Harakhty|Harakhty]], the falcon of eternal beauty." 4. Isis: "Festival of Isis, the child in her nest." 5. Nephthys: "Festival of the pure fish before the barque of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|qid=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}}." The [[Akrobatentruppe festival calendar]] from the 35th regnal year of [[Senusret III]] situates the intercalary days at the end of the [[Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] season, serving as a "year closer." These days were sometimes marked as special feasts on [[diagonal star clocks]]. === New Kingdom === During the [[:en:Ramesside period|Ramesside period]], the intercalary days were reassigned. For instance, the fifth intercalary day became a dual celebration for [[Ra-Harakhty]] and {{ill|Nephthys|en|Nephthys|qid=Q169040|lt=Nephthys|1=Nephthys}}, occurring on the first day of the first [[Achet|Achet month]]. Meanwhile, Seth’s day shifted from the fifth to the third day. Both {{ill|Alan Gardiner|en|Alan Gardiner|qid=Q441060|lt=Alan Gardiner|1=Alan Gardiner}} and [[Richard-Anthony Parker]] theorized that the months shifted approximately 30 days over the centuries due to the heliacal rising of Sirius gradually moving from early July earlier in the year. These shifts affected the alignment of Heriu-renpet with the solar year. In 237 BCE, the [[:en:Canopus Decree|Canopus Decree]] added the birthday of [[:en:Ptolemy III|Ptolemy III]] as a sixth intercalary day, aligning it directly after the fourth month of Schemu. === Later Assignments of Days === * '''First day:''' Birthday of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|qid=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}} * '''Second day:''' Birthday of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|qid=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}} * '''Third day:''' Birthday of [[Seth (Egyptian mythology)|Seth]] * '''Fourth day:''' Birthday of {{ill|Isis|en|Isis|qid=Q79876|lt=Isis|1=Isis}} * '''Fifth day:''' Birthday of {{ill|Nephthys|en|Nephthys|qid=Q169040|lt=Nephthys|1=Nephthys}} * '''Sixth day (from 237 BCE):''' Birthday of [[:en:Ptolemy III|Ptolemy III]] (October 18) == See Also == - [[Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar]] - [[Sothic cycle]] == Literature == * [[:en:Rolf Krauss|Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|qid=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker|1=Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz / Wiesbaden 1950. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Wein, Weib und Gesang – Rituale für die Gefährliche Göttin.'' In: Carola Metzner-Nebelsick: ''Rituale in der Vorgeschichte, Antike und Gegenwart.'' Leidorf, Rahden 2003, pp. 47–48. {{NaviBlock |Navigationsleiste Egyptian Calendar Months to the Beginning of the New Kingdom |Navigationsleiste Egyptian Calendar Months from the Beginning of the New Kingdom }} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Heriu-renpet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Heriu-renpet]] {{wikidata link|Q1610070}} [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
